subject: RE: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event
body:
Jeffrey,
 
I want to apologize for Friday!  I was such a mess and I really didn't
mean to break down in front of you like that...but I also so much
appreciate your understanding, kind words and actions.  I am doing SO
MUCH BETTER emotionally and physcially.  Ike and I had a well needed
'conversation' Friday evening and I have been off the percosets!  I
think the doctor was completely right in denying me more medication...I
needed to stop acting like 'super woman' and take it easy.  My swelling
has gone down a lot since Friday and I've been keeping my arms down...so
feeling better and not as sore.  I think I was trying to 'over-do' in an
effort to prove 'something' to Ike...I guess I like to be the caregiver
and not the caretaker!  Anyway, that is over with!
 
If you are not going to be in the office this week, I was hoping I could
stay home tomorrow and possibly Wed. too (my doctor has requested this
of me)  By Thursday, I'm sure I will be ready to come in because I will
be feeling loads more like myself.!
 
AND....I'm loving my tits~~~~~!!!  They still are too swollen up top and
on the sides...but I can tell...they're going to be great.
 
AND!!  I'm so incredibly happy that you are not reporting to jail today!

 
xoxoxo!!
 
Les
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Sunday, June 01, 2008 6:51 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event


les  how are you?


----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Sunday, June 1, 2008 11:10:33 AM
Subject: RE: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event


I have relayed!
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com] 
Sent: Saturday, May 31, 2008 8:19 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event


a production , not a fair , certainly not science but entertainment,,
Brians wife is a television producer..


----- Original Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: J. Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Saturday, May 31, 2008 12:16:50 PM
Subject: FW: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Saturday, May 31, 2008 9:37 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event



Dear Jeffrey,

Thank you so very much for your invitation to breakfast, which Lesley
kindly conveyed via email and telephone yesterday.

Sorry I wasn't in town - The World Science Festival music/brain event
swayed way to far from science and the vision I formulated when the
organizers first consulted me back in October.  Originally, it was
supposed to take place at Lincoln Ctr, but that fell through.  They
managed to get another landmark location for the event, but the deal
they had to cut with the devil (no pun intended) was that the Abyssian
Baptist Church controlled the venue.  Consequently, what was supposed to
be 60 - 90 mins of neuroscience and musicology turned into a potpourri
of musical performances, church/ethnic history, fluffy patient anecdotes
by Sacks, and only a dollup of real science by yours truly - not
something I wanted to be connected with.  I didn't get the
minute-by-minute schedule from the choirmaster (!) until after Memorial
Day and my invitation to you, so you can imagine my surprise when I got
the rundown. [It's not just that I'm an anti-theist who would have run
the risk of saying something about the merits of hard-core empiricism
and the dangers of blind faith with Pastor Calvin Butts sitting nearby
on stage! (To my knowledge, Rev Jeremiah Wright was not going to fly in
from Chicago, but there's a decent chance Al Sharpton will be there.)]

I'm wondering what you thought of some of these physics/math-oriented
venues?  The one on dance inspired by Brian Greene's book struck me as,
well, weird.  Alan Alda's involved elsewhere.  Seems more like a
Discovery Channel fair than a true World Science Festival.  Are you
going to any of the other events?

Really, I'm happy to come down any time to breakfast - would be great to
see you again and catch up, and I could drop in on my octogenarian mom
in the Bronx afterwards.

Peace & Love,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
cellphone 781-718-6390
body_html:
Jeffrey,
 
I want to apologize for Friday!  I was such a mess and I really didn't mean to break down in front of you like that...but I also so much appreciate your understanding, kind words and actions.  I am doing SO MUCH BETTER emotionally and physcially.  Ike and I had a well needed 'conversation' Friday evening and I have been off the percosets!  I think the doctor was completely right in denying me more medication...I needed to stop acting like 'super woman' and take it easy.  My swelling has gone down a lot since Friday and I've been keeping my arms down...so feeling better and not as sore.  I think I was trying to 'over-do' in an effort to prove 'something' to Ike...I guess I like to be the caregiver and not the caretaker!  Anyway, that is over with!
 
If you are not going to be in the office this week, I was hoping I could stay home tomorrow and possibly Wed. too (my doctor has requested this of me)  By Thursday, I'm sure I will be ready to come in because I will be feeling loads more like myself.!
 
AND....I'm loving my tits~~~~~!!!  They still are too swollen up top and on the sides...but I can tell...they're going to be great.
 
AND!!  I'm so incredibly happy that you are not reporting to jail today! 
 
xoxoxo!!
 
Les
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To JE: Thanks; Abyssinian event

Dear Jeffrey,

Thank you so very much for your invitation to breakfast, which Lesley kindly conveyed via email and telephone yesterday.

Sorry I wasn't in town - The World Science Festival music/brain event swayed way to far from science and the vision I formulated when the organizers first consulted me back in October.  Originally, it was supposed to take place at Lincoln Ctr, but that fell through.  They managed to get another landmark location for the event, but the deal they had to cut with the devil (no pun intended) was that the Abyssian Baptist Church controlled the venue.  Consequently, what was supposed to be 60 - 90 mins of neuroscience and musicology turned into a potpourri of musical performances, church/ethnic history, fluffy patient anecdotes by Sacks, and only a dollup of real science by yours truly - not something I wanted to be connected with.  I didn't get the minute-by-minute schedule from the choirmaster (!) until after Memorial Day and my invitation to you, so you can imagine my surprise when I got the rundown. [It's not just that I'm an anti-theist who would have run the risk of saying something about the merits of hard-core empiricism and the dangers of blind faith with Pastor Calvin Butts sitting nearby on stage! (To my knowledge, Rev Jeremiah Wright was not going to fly in from Chicago, but there's a decent chance Al Sharpton will be there.)]

I'm wondering what you thought of some of these physics/math-oriented venues?  The one on dance inspired by Brian Greene's book struck me as, well, weird.  Alan Alda's involved elsewhere.  Seems more like a Discovery Channel fair than a true World Science Festival.  Are you going to any of the other events?

Really, I'm happy to come down any time to breakfast - would be great to see you again and catch up, and I could drop in on my octogenarian mom in the Bronx afterwards.

Peace & Love,
Mark

cellphone 781-718-6390
